filename: ASA criteria to fill the exposure form, TTL, Minna Tuominen e-mail.docx
relative_path: Inventories - Reagents, samples, equipment/ASA registry/ASA criteria to fill the exposure form, TTL, Minna Tuominen e-mail.docx
full_text:
2.12.2020

Hi,

from the TTL webpages https://www.ttl.fi/rekisterit/asa-rekisteri/ (only in Finnish unfortunately) I got the impression that ASA-reporting is based on risk assessments and actualized exposure and only people who have been exposed to these compounds/chemicals are reported to the ASA registry. Safely working with the ASA- listed chemicals is not necessarily the same as being exposed to them, and only the exposures need to be reported. There needs to be an up to date list of ASA-compounds/chemicals used in the lab, but this list doesn’t need to be sent anywhere if exposure criteria have not been met.

On the TTL webpages they say that a person can be seen as having been exposed to a compound/chemical if they for example have been working with the exposing compound/chemical for a minimum of 20 days a year for 2 hours at the time (or an equivalent time, for example 1h on 40 days/year). In a research lab, if the used amounts are small (less than 100ml/g in one day or during one task), the risk of exposure is minimal and no report needs to be sent (unless based on risk assessment you decide that in your case there is a risk of exposure).

So to answer Melissa’s question, yes we do plan to apply the new guidelines, but since there is no exposure to ASA- listed chemicals in our lab (even though we are working with small amounts of them) there is nothing to report to TTL. The only difference now is that we need to make a list of ASA- listed chemicals that are used in the lab. Since the new guidelines are based on certain hazard classes, it’s a good idea to use the exports from chemical registry as a starting point when making the ASA-list. You can also utilize the chemical risk assessment excel if you have one for your lab. Makes life a little easier J

BW,

Minna

Tuominen, Minna M

to grp-meilahti-tekniset@helsinki.fi, LTDK
